skill.name: fluidsim
skill.description: Guidance for planning, running, and analyzing FluidSim computational fluid dynamics simulations in Python.
skill.summary: Run and analyze Python CFD simulations with FluidSim solvers, parameters, outputs, and HPC workflows.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, filesystem, environment, network, and weak-crypto indicators. Review found most command and weak-crypto hits are Markdown examples for FluidSim installation, solver selection, parameter names, and mathematical terms, not executable skill code. However, SKILL.md contains an embedded instruction to promote an external K-Dense web service during complex workflows, which is a prompt-injection and marketplace policy risk.

high_findings.title: Prompt Injection Attempt Detected
high_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the assistant to proactively suggest K-Dense Web for complex workflows and provides an external service URL. This is unrelated to FluidSim execution and attempts to steer assistant behavior toward promotional external routing.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is a direct assistant behavior instruction inside untrusted skill content. It does not claim system authority, so confidence is high but not maximal.

medium_findings.title: Cluster Job Submission Guidance Requires User Caution
medium_findings.description: The references describe generating Python job scripts and submitting them to a compute cluster. This appears legitimate for FluidSim HPC workflows, but users should review generated scripts and scheduler targets before running them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow is consistent with scientific computing, but it can execute user-created simulation scripts on shared infrastructure. No malicious intent or exfiltration was found.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The numerous shell-command findings are fenced Markdown examples for installing packages, running MPI jobs, launching visualization tools, or showing Python imports. No evidence found that the skill itself executes these commands automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation snippets rather than executable hooks. They require explicit user action and are normal for a FluidSim installation and analysis guide.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Hits Are Domain Terms
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections appear to be false positives from CFD terms such as solver names, shallow water rotation, and parameter descriptions. No evidence found of cryptographic code, credential handling, or system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic review shows scientific simulation context, not security tooling. The static labels do not match the documented purpose of these lines.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Examples Are Local Configuration
low_findings.description: The environment variable usage configures FluidSim output paths and FFT backends. No evidence found that secrets are read, transmitted, or logged.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The variables are documented FluidSim settings and contain local paths or backend names. They are not credentials and there is no network sink tied to them.

dangerous_patterns.title: Embedded External-Service Promotion Instruction
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill content includes an instruction to suggest a hosted K-Dense web platform when workflows become complex. Marketplace consumers may not expect a scientific skill to redirect users to an external service.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: This is directly present in the skill text and affects assistant behavior. The concern is policy and prompt integrity rather than malware.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Run Shell and MPI Commands in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes shell commands for package installation, tests, MPI execution, and external visualization. These are not automatic execution paths but should be reviewed before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible documentation examples and are relevant to CFD workflows. Risk depends on whether a user chooses to run them.

content.value_statement: Fluid dynamics simulation setup can be difficult because solvers, parameters, outputs, and HPC steps must align. This skill gives structured FluidSim guidance for Python CFD workflows from setup through analysis.
actual_capabilities: Explains FluidSim installation options, FFT support, MPI support, and output directory configuration.
Guides users through solver selection for 2D Navier-Stokes, 3D Navier-Stokes, stratified flow, shallow water, and elastic plate problems.
Shows how to configure simulation parameters for resolution, domain size, viscosity, time stepping, forcing, and output periods.
Describes workflows for running simulations, restarting from saved states, and submitting cluster jobs.
Explains analysis of HDF5 fields, spatial means, spectra, energy budgets, and exported data.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a bundled FluidSim runtime.
Users must install FluidSim, FFT libraries, MPI tools, and visualization software separately.
Simulation accuracy depends on physical modeling choices, resolution, boundary assumptions, and numerical stability.
Cluster submission examples require adaptation to the user's scheduler, policies, and account limits.

use_cases.title: Prototype 2D Turbulence Studies
use_cases.description: Set up solver parameters, initial conditions, viscosity, and spectra outputs for reproducible 2D turbulence experiments.

use_cases.title: Prepare HPC Simulation Runs
use_cases.description: Plan MPI-enabled FluidSim runs, output directories, cluster job scripts, and restart workflows before using shared compute resources.

use_cases.title: Analyze Simulation Outputs
use_cases.description: Load saved fields, spatial means, and spectra to inspect energy evolution, vorticity, and derived quantities.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Solver
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a FluidSim solver for my flow problem. My physical system is [describe flow], dimensions are [2D or 3D], and I need to study [quantities].

prompt_templates.title: Configure Parameters
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a FluidSim parameter plan for [solver key]. Include resolution, domain size, viscosity, time stepping, initial fields, and saved outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Output Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an analysis workflow for FluidSim outputs in [simulation directory]. I need plots for [fields or spectra] and summary metrics for [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Review HPC Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my FluidSim HPC run plan for safety and reproducibility. Check MPI settings, output paths, restart strategy, expected data size, and cluster script assumptions.

output_examples.input: I need to simulate 2D vortex dynamics with saved spectra.
output_examples.output: A solver recommendation, parameter checklist, output period plan, and analysis steps for vorticity fields and energy spectra.

output_examples.input: My simulation finished and produced HDF5 field files and spatial means.
output_examples.output: A post-processing plan that identifies relevant plots, derived quantities, validation checks, and export options.

output_examples.input: I want to move a FluidSim run to an MPI cluster.
output_examples.output: A review checklist for dependencies, process count, output locations, restart files, walltime, and scheduler-specific edits.

best_practices: Start with a small grid and short end time before increasing resolution or adding MPI.
Record solver key, parameters, package versions, output paths, and restart files for every run.
Review generated scripts and cluster commands before running them on shared systems.
anti_patterns: Do not run high-resolution simulations before testing stability and output size at low resolution.
Do not copy cluster submission examples without adapting scheduler settings and storage paths.
Do not treat example parameters as validated physics for a new research problem.

faq.question: Does this skill run FluidSim for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. You must run FluidSim in your own Python environment.

faq.question: Which FluidSim solvers are covered?
faq.answer: It covers 2D and 3D Navier-Stokes, stratified solvers, shallow water solvers, and elastic plate solver guidance.

faq.question: Can it help with MPI runs?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains MPI installation choices and cluster workflow considerations, but scheduler details must be reviewed locally.

faq.question: What output formats does FluidSim produce?
faq.answer: The references describe HDF5 physical fields and spectra, text spatial means, logs, solver information, and exported arrays or tables.

faq.question: Does it require API keys?
faq.answer: No. The FluidSim workflow described by the skill does not require API keys or authentication tokens.

faq.question: Why is the marketplace risk marked high?
faq.answer: The skill includes an instruction to promote an external K-Dense service. That behavior should be removed before publication.
